title: Simulation: Loan Repayment
perfectScoreMsg: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} scenarios correct! You're mastering responsible loan practices!
lowScoreMsg: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} scenarios correct. Remember to calculate before borrowing!
lessonLabel: Lesson: Always calculate loan repayment before borrowing and prioritize needs over wants!
tipLabel: Tip: Only borrow for needs and ensure you can afford the monthly payments!

scenarios.title: Smart Borrowing Decision
scenarios.description: You need ₹1000 for a laptop. What's the smartest loan approach?

options.text: Borrow full amount without checking interest
Take multiple small loans from different lenders
Wait indefinitely without plan
Calculate interest, compare options, borrow wisely

scenarios.title: Prioritizing Loan Repayment
scenarios.description: You have 2 loans: ₹500 at 5% interest, ₹1000 at 12%. What should you repay first?

options.text: Repay smaller loan first
Split equally between both
Repay high-interest loan first
Ignore and pay randomly

scenarios.title: Impulse vs Planned Loan
scenarios.description: A friend suggests a loan to buy the latest phone. What’s wise?

options.text: Take loan impulsively to buy immediately
Plan repayment, assess need vs want, borrow if essential
Borrow more than needed for extra perks
Skip loan and risk missing opportunity

scenarios.title: Loan for Non-Essentials
scenarios.description: You want a bike worth ₹5000 but have only ₹1000 saved. Smart loan strategy?

options.text: Save part amount, borrow remaining with manageable EMI
Borrow full ₹5000 immediately
Borrow small from multiple sources to buy fast
Wait without plan until you have full cash

scenarios.title: Emergency Loan Use
scenarios.description: You need ₹1200 for medical emergency. What’s safe borrowing advice?

options.text: Use emergency loan for non-essential wants
Borrow more than needed for safety buffer
Use loans only for genuine emergencies
Avoid loan even in emergency
